structured.name: Cold Boot Charging Station - Rejected
structured.objective: To inform the Charging Station that its not (yet) accepted by the CSMS: Rejected status.
structured.description:
This use case describes the behavior of the CSMS and a Charging Station, when the Charging
Station is informed by the CSMS that it is not (yet) accepted using the Rejected status.
scenario: 1. The Charging Station is powered up.

2. The Charging Station sends BootNotificationRequest to the CSMS.
3 The CSMS responds with BootNotificationResponse with the status Rejected to the Charging
Station.

4. The Charging Station will resend BootNotificationRequest after the number of seconds
indicated by the interval field. (Interval from BootNotificationResponse).
structured.prerequisites:
1. The CSMS requires to set the Charging Station in the Rejected status.
2. The Charging Station is powered down.
structured.postconditions: The Charging Station remains in the Rejected status.
structured.error_handling:
When there is no response or a time-out from the CSMS: The Charging Station resends
BootNotificationRequest after a waiting interval. This waiting interval can be based on the interval
from a previous BootNotificationResponse or chosen by the Charging Station itself. In the latter
case, the Charging Station chooses this interval in a way that avoids flooding the CSMS with
requests.
structured.remarks:
During the status Rejected, the Charging Station may no longer be reachable from the CSMS. The
Charging Station MAY e.g. close its communication channel or shut down its communication
hardware.
Additionally, the CSMS MAY close the communication channel, for instance to free up system
resources.
It is advised not to accept any transactions until the BootNotification of the Charging Station has
been accepted by the CSMS. See: Transactions before being accepted by a CSMS
When the CSMS returns with BootNotificationResponse with the status Accepted, B01 - Cold Boot
Charging Station applies.

requirements.precondition:
If the Charging Station is configured to accept
Transactions before being accepted by a CSMS
requirements.definition: The Charging Station MAY allow locally authorized transactions.

requirements.precondition:
If the CSMS returns the status Rejected. For
example when a Charging Station is blacklisted.
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL NOT send any OCPP message to
the CSMS until the retry interval has expired.

requirements.precondition:
When the CSMS has Rejected the
BootNotificationRequest from the Charging
Station.
requirements.definition: The CSMS SHALL NOT initiate any messages.

The Charging Station MAY close the connection until it needs to
send the next BootNotificationRequest.

requirements.precondition:
If the interval in the BootNotificationResponse
equals 0, and the status is other than Accepted
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL choose a waiting interval on its
own, in a way that avoids flooding the CSMS with requests.

requirements.precondition:
If the interval in the BootNotificationResponse is
greater than 0, and the status is other than
Accepted
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL send a BootNotificationRequest
after the set interval has passed.

requirements.precondition:
B03.FR.03
AND
Charging Station sends a message that is not a
BootNotificationRequest
requirements.definition:
CSMS SHALL respond with RPC Framework: CALLERROR:
SecurityError.

requirements.precondition:
B03.FR.03
AND
CSMS sends a message that is not a response
to a BootNotificationRequest from Charging
Station
requirements.definition:
Charging Station SHALL respond with RPC Framework:
CALLERROR: SecurityError.

chunks.content:
# B03 - Cold Boot Charging Station - Rejected

**Objective:** To inform the Charging Station that its not (yet) accepted by the CSMS: Rejected status.

**Description:** This use case describes the behavior of the CSMS and a Charging Station, when the Charging
Station is informed by the CSMS that it is not (yet) accepted using the Rejected status.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The Charging Station is powered up.
2. The Charging Station sends BootNotificationRequest to the CSMS.
3 The CSMS responds with BootNotificationResponse with the status Rejected to the Charging
Station.
4. The Charging Station will resend BootNotificationRequest after the number of seconds
indicated by the interval field. (Interval from BootNotificationResponse).

**Prerequisites:** 1. The CSMS requires to set the Charging Station in the Rejected status.
2. The Charging Station is powered down.

**Postconditions:** The Charging Station remains in the Rejected status.

**Error handling:** When there is no response or a time-out from the CSMS: The Charging Station resends
BootNotificationRequest after a waiting interval. This waiting interval can be based on the interval
from a previous BootNotificationResponse or chosen by the Charging Station itself. In the latter
case, the Charging Station chooses this interval in a way that avoids flooding the CSMS with
requests.

**Remarks:** During the status Rejected, the Charging Station may no longer be reachable from the CSMS. The
Charging Station MAY e.g. close its communication channel or shut down its communication
hardware.
Additionally, the CSMS MAY close the communication channel, for instance to free up system
resources.
It is advised not to accept any transactions until the BootNotification of the Charging Station has
been accepted by the CSMS. See: Transactions before being accepted by a CSMS
When the CSMS returns with BootNotificationResponse with the status Accepted, B01 - Cold Boot
Charging Station applies.

metadata.heading: B03 - Cold Boot Charging Station - Rejected

chunks.content:
# B03 - Requirements

**B03.FR.01**
Precondition: If the Charging Station is configured to accept
Transactions before being accepted by a CSMS
The Charging Station MAY allow locally authorized transactions.

**B03.FR.02**
Precondition: If the CSMS returns the status Rejected. For
example when a Charging Station is blacklisted.
The Charging Station SHALL NOT send any OCPP message to
the CSMS until the retry interval has expired.

**B03.FR.03**
Precondition: When the CSMS has Rejected the
BootNotificationRequest from the Charging
Station.
The CSMS SHALL NOT initiate any messages.

**B03.FR.04**
Precondition: B03.FR.03
The Charging Station MAY close the connection until it needs to
send the next BootNotificationRequest.

**B03.FR.05**
Precondition: If the interval in the BootNotificationResponse
equals 0, and the status is other than Accepted
The Charging Station SHALL choose a waiting interval on its
own, in a way that avoids flooding the CSMS with requests.

**B03.FR.06**
Precondition: If the interval in the BootNotificationResponse is
greater than 0, and the status is other than
Accepted
The Charging Station SHALL send a BootNotificationRequest
after the set interval has passed.

**B03.FR.07**
Precondition: B03.FR.03
AND
Charging Station sends a message that is not a
BootNotificationRequest
CSMS SHALL respond with RPC Framework: CALLERROR:
SecurityError.

**B03.FR.08**
Precondition: B03.FR.03
AND
CSMS sends a message that is not a response
to a BootNotificationRequest from Charging
Station
Charging Station SHALL respond with RPC Framework:
CALLERROR: SecurityError.
